claim: On March 13, 1962, the Joint Chiefs of Staff submitted to Secretary of Defense Robert McNamara a top-secret memorandum titled 'Justification for U.S. Military Intervention in Cuba (TS)' — colloquially known as Operation Northwoods. The proposal detailed pretexts for war with Cuba, including: remote-controlling civilian aircraft repainted as U.S. Air Force planes, fabricating a 'shoot-down' of a U.S. fighter, sinking boats of Cuban refugees on the high seas, exploding a U.S. ship, and 'orchestrating terrorism in U.S. cities.' The proposal was rejected by President John F. Kennedy. The document was declassified on November 18, 1997 by the JFK Assassination Records Review Board.

sources.label: National Security Archive (George Washington University) — Chairman, Joint Chiefs of Staff, Memorandum to the Secretary of Defense, “Justification for US Military Intervention in Cuba,” TOP SECRET SPECIAL HANDLING NOFORN, 13 March 1962
sources.quote: Justification for US Military Intervention in Cuba

sources.label: U.S. National Archives Catalog — Operation Northwoods (declassified holdings)
Haley Press / @strategichaley, Instagram reel DVOtv6QCTFf (Feb 26, 2026) — surfacing source

notes: National Security Archive (GWU) is the authoritative declassified-documents host. The Northwoods proposal is a real, declassified JCS memo. The reel invokes it to frame '9/11 planes were remote controlled'; Northwoods is documentary evidence of what was once *proposed*, not evidence that any such operation was executed on 9/11. Anchored under y1947 (CIA founded) as the closest existing chapter; consider a future y1962 or y1947 split if more Cold War records accumulate.
